curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 15 chapters, and 26 topics extracted through raw extraction, heading classification, normalization, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Prove the irrationality of specific numbers (e.g., √2, √3).
Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.
Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.
Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.

topics: Introduction to rational numbers
Representation of rational numbers on the number line
Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
Decimal representation of rational numbers
Computing decimal expansions systematically
Reasoning about numbers using step-by-step logical procedures
Representing rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models

units.description: Introduction to Polynomials, Sequences and Progressions, Exploring Algebraic Identities, Linear Equations in Two Variables
topics: Algebraic expressions, Definition of polynomial, Degree, Linear polynomials and applications
Sequences: Explicit/Recursive rules, Arithmetic Progressions (AP), Geometric Progressions (GP)
Fractals, Tower of Hanoi puzzle
Revisiting identities, Factorisation using identities, Visualising factorisation with algebra tiles
Linear equations in two variables: Graphical representation, Slope-intercept form, Pair of linear equations
Brief history of coordinate geometry
The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system
Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
Midpoint of the line-segment

units.description: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates, Lines and Angles, Triangles – Congruence Theorems, 4-gons (Quadrilaterals), Circles
topics: History of geometry, Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Euclid's definitions and postulates
Rays, angles, intersecting lines, parallelism theorems (linear pair, vertically opposite)
Congruence conditions: SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS; Isosceles triangles
Parallelograms properties, Midpoint theorem, Central symmetry
Circle definitions, Chords, Angles subtended by arcs, Cyclic quadrilaterals

units.description: Area and Perimeter, Surface Area and Volume
topics: Perimeter of shapes, Introduction to Pi (irrationality), Length of an arc
Heron's formula, Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals
Area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres
Graphical representation of data (stacked bar graphs)
Measures of central tendency: Mean, Median, Mode, Weighted average
Concept of probability and randomness
Empirical vs Theoretical probability
Introduction to rational numbers
Representation of rational numbers on the number line
Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
Decimal representation of rational numbers
Introduction to irrational numbers
Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3
The square root spiral
subtopics: Reasoning about numbers using step-by-step logical procedures
Representing rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models

topics: Algebraic expressions, Definition of polynomial, Degree, Linear polynomials and applications
Sequences: Explicit/Recursive rules, Arithmetic Progressions (AP), Geometric Progressions (GP)
Fractals, Tower of Hanoi puzzle
Revisiting identities, Factorisation using identities, Visualising factorisation with algebra tiles
Linear equations in two variables: Graphical representation, Slope-intercept form, Pair of linear equations
key_concepts: Consistency and inconsistency of linear equations

topics: Brief history of coordinate geometry
The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system
Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
Midpoint of the line-segment
subtopics: Specifying locations and relative positions using coordinates
Representing floor plans on a grid
Checking collinearity and right-angled triangles via coordinates

explicit_chapters: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
topics: History of geometry, Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Euclid's definitions and postulates
Rays, angles, intersecting lines, parallelism theorems (linear pair, vertically opposite)
Congruence conditions: SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS; Isosceles triangles
Parallelograms properties, Midpoint theorem, Central symmetry
Circle definitions, Chords, Angles subtended by arcs, Cyclic quadrilaterals
subtopics: Axiomatic structure and proofs without words
Proofs using Euclid's axioms for triangles/quadrilaterals/circles

topics: Perimeter of shapes, Introduction to Pi (irrationality), Length of an arc
Heron's formula, Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals
Area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres
subtopics: Generalisation vs special cases in formulae

topics: Graphical representation of data (stacked bar graphs)
Measures of central tendency: Mean, Median, Mode, Weighted average
Concept of probability and randomness
Empirical vs Theoretical probability
subtopics: Tree diagrams and tables for events
Simulation and pattern recognition in random experiments

practicals.title: Lab Practical (Mathematics Laboratory)
practicals.source_text: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books. Prescribed Books: Mathematics - Textbook for class IX, Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX, Laboratory Manual - Mathematics

activities.title: Geometric Constructions and Manipulation Activities
activities.source_text: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras; Construct different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons. Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.source_text: Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.source_text: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.

activities.title: Fractal Analysis using GP
activities.source_text: Analyse attributes of fractals using Geometric Progressions. Visualise a GP graphically.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation
activities.source_text: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.

projects.title: Continuous and Holistic Assessment Projects
projects.source_text: Projects, activities, and investigations form an integral part of the learning process. Portfolio (05 Marks) is a component of Internal Assessment.

marks_distribution: - Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 10 Marks
teacher_notes: Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.
Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.
Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.
Focus on real-life applications, experiential learning, and cross-disciplinary contexts.
Emphasis on computational thinking: breaking down problems into sub-problems, algorithmic problem-solving, and visualisation.
Assessment includes formative components (projects, activities, investigations) alongside summative examinations.

classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit covering rational and irrational numbers, their representation on the number line, density proofs, decimal expansions, square root spiral construction.
Topic introducing the concept of rational numbers as a subset of real numbers.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Representation of rational numbers on the number line
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Topic detailing methods to locate and represent specific rational values along a continuous number axis.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Density of rational numbers and its proof
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Topic explaining the property that between any two distinct rationals there exists another rational.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Topic covering algorithms to generate intermediate rational values.
Topic discussing terminating and non-terminating recurring decimal expansions.
Topic defining irrational numbers as non-recurring, non-terminating decimals.
Topic presenting logical proofs demonstrating that square roots of non-perfect squares are irrational.
Topic describing the geometric construction of a spiral representing successive square roots.
Unit covering polynomials, sequences (AP/GP), algebraic identities, and linear equations in two variables.
Topic defining algebraic expressions, polynomials, degree, and linear applications.
Topic covering explicit/recursive rules for Arithmetic (AP) and Geometric (GP) progressions.
Topic revisiting identities and visualizing factorization using algebra tiles.
Topic covering graphical representation, slope-intercept form, and pairs of linear equations.
Topic exploring recursive patterns and fractal geometry through classic puzzles.
Unit covering the history of coordinate geometry, Cartesian systems, distance formula, and midpoint calculations.
Topic providing historical context for the development of coordinate systems.
Topic defining axes, quadrants, and plotting points in a two-dimensional plane.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Topic deriving and applying the distance formula for any pair of coordinates.
Topic explaining how to calculate the center point between two coordinates.
Unit covering Euclid's axioms, lines and angles, triangle congruence theorems, quadrilaterals, and circle properties.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Topic discussing the history of geometry, Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, and foundational definitions.
Topic covering rays, intersecting lines, parallelism theorems, linear pairs, and vertically opposite angles.
Topic detailing SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS conditions and isosceles triangle properties.
Topic exploring parallelogram properties, midpoint theorem, and central symmetry.
Topic defining circles, chords, angles subtended by arcs, and cyclic quadrilaterals.
Unit covering perimeter and area concepts, Heron's formula, surface areas, and volumes of 3D solids.
Topic discussing perimeter calculations, introduction to Pi, arc length, Heron's formula, and Brahmagupta's formula.
Topic covering calculations for cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres, sectors, and segments.
Unit covering graphical data representation, measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode), probability concepts, and empirical vs theoretical probability.
Topic focusing on stacked bar graphs and measures of central tendency including weighted averages.
Topic explaining randomness, probability scales, tree diagrams, and simulation of random experiments.

teacher_notes: Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.
Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.
Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.
Focus on real-life applications, experiential learning, and cross-disciplinary contexts.
Emphasis on computational thinking: breaking down problems into sub-problems, algorithmic problem-solving, and visualisation.
Assessment includes formative components (projects, activities, investigations) alongside summative examinations.

Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.

competency_groups.title: Understanding Numbers and Real Number Properties
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers systematically.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers and prove their irrationality (e.g., √2, √3).
Construct the square root spiral using algorithms and visual models.

competency_groups.title: Algebraic Reasoning and Modelling with Equations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; models and solves contextualised problems using equations.
competencies: Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression and define a polynomial (degree, terms, coefficients).
Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.
Identify patterns in linear relationships and visualise them via slope-intercept form.
Graph linear equations and solve pairs of linear equations graphically.
Solve systems of linear equations using substitution and elimination methods.
Determine the nature of solutions (consistency/inconsistency) for pairs of linear equations.
Model daily-life phenomena using graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Polynomials / Linear Equations in Two Variables

competency_groups.title: Pattern Recognition and Sequence Modelling
competency_groups.description: Identifies patterns, determines recursive/explicit rules for sequences, and applies them to real-world contexts like fractals.
competencies: Understand the concept of a sequence and identify its pattern.
Determine explicit (general) and recursive rules for different sequences.
Obtain terms of a sequence given its rule or vice versa.
Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP) and Geometric Progressions (GP), determining their nth terms.
Visualise APs and GPs graphically.
Analyse attributes of fractals using GP concepts.
Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle by writing step-by-step rules.

competency_groups.title: Factorisation and Algebraic Identity Visualisation
competency_groups.description: Visualises identities using geometric models, factorises expressions, and simplifies rational expressions.
competencies: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models or algebra tiles.
Determine factors of algebraic expressions using identities.
Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.
Find simplified versions of rational expressions.
Use computational thinking strategies (decomposition, step-by-step procedures) to visualise and factorise.

competency_groups.title: Geometric Reasoning and Congruence Proofs
competency_groups.description: Describes relationships including congruence, proves theorems using Euclid's axioms for triangles/quadrilaterals.
competencies: Explain that a triangle is rigid unlike quadrilaterals; identify uses of rigidity.
Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent and identify correspondence.
Use SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions correctly.
Prove basic properties of isosceles triangles.
Explain propositions and their converses; use counter-examples (e.g., SSA exceptions).
Frame precise definitions for 4-gons/parallelograms.
Prove characterisations of parallelograms, midpoint theorem, and medians concurrency.

competency_groups.title: Axiomatic Structure and Geometric Constructions
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements using axioms/postulates; carries out geometric constructions satisfying constraints.
competencies: Describe the history of geometry from ancient civilisations to Euclid.
State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry and define parallelism.
Justify simple constructions (e.g., square construction via Sulbasutras) using axioms.
Explain angles, rays, linear pairs, vertically opposite angles, and transitivity of parallelism.
Follow proof by contradiction in geometry.
Construct bisectors, triangles, polygons satisfying given constraints.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates / Lines and Angles

competency_groups.title: Circle Theorems and Properties Verification
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems about circle geometry including chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons.
competencies: State definitions of circle terms: chord, diameter, radius, arc, segment, sector.
Explain why a unique circle exists through three non-collinear points (circumcircle).
Prove relationships between chords and angles subtended at the centre/remaining part.
Determine conditions for concyclic points and cyclic quadrilaterals.
Apply computational thinking to break down circle problems into systematic steps.

competency_groups.title: Spatial Relationships and Coordinate Modelling
competency_groups.description: Specifies locations, describes spatial relationships using coordinates; plots linear equations.
competencies: Specify locations relative to another point using coordinates.
Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.
Compute distance between two points in the 2-D plane.
Determine if three points are collinear or form a right-angled triangle.
Compute midpoint of a line segment and fourth vertex of a parallelogram.

competency_groups.title: Area Derivation and Formula Application
competency_groups.description: Visualises, represents, and calculates area of plane figures using Heron's formula and Brahmagupta's generalisation.
competencies: Define perimeter as the length around a shape boundary.
Explain constant ratio of circumference to diameter (irrationality of Pi).
Compute arc lengths and areas of sectors/segments.
Apply Heron's formula for triangle area from side lengths.
State Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals; explain it as a generalisation of Heron's.
Explain 'squaring' shapes (e.g., Baudhayana) and historical approximations to Pi.

competency_groups.title: Surface Area and Volume of Solids
competency_groups.description: Visualises and uses mathematical thinking to discover formulae for surface areas/volumes of solid objects.
competencies: Compute surface area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres.
Recognise special cases (e.g., cube as a special cuboid).
Compare surface areas and volumes by varying dimensions systematically.

competency_groups.title: Data Analysis and Central Tendency
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendencies; interprets statistical data to answer investigative questions.
competencies: Collect, organise, visualise, and interpret data using stacked bar graphs.
Compute mean, median, mode, and weighted average for decision-making.
Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life datasets.

competency_groups.title: Randomness and Likelihood Estimation
competency_groups.description: Applies concepts from probability to solve problems on likelihood of everyday events.
competencies: Understand the concept of randomness and describe likelihood using a scale.
Estimate empirical probability by analysing data or performing experiments.
Define theoretical probability based on sample space and events.
Compute probabilities using tree diagrams and tables.

competency_groups.title: Algorithmic Problem Solving and Decomposition
competency_groups.description: Deals with complex problems by breaking them down into simple sub-problems; designs algorithms.
competencies: Decompose a problem into manageable sub-problems across all units.
Describe and analyse sequences of instructions (algorithms) for number representation, geometric proofs, or mensuration calculations.
Analyse similarities/differences among problems to create general procedures.
Engage in algorithmic problem-solving to design solutions for pattern recognition, simulation, and visualisation.

competency_weightage: Deep conceptual understanding and logical reasoning prioritized over rote learning.
Competency-based education focusing on problem-solving, visualisation, modelling, communication, computational thinking, and data analytics.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material; demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge and techniques in a different way.
Examine information into parts; present opinions based on criteria; compile information to propose alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.component: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment

practical_assessment.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities
practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)
Mathematics exemplar problems for class IX (NCERT)

project_assessment.title: Continuous and Holistic Assessment Projects
project_assessment.description: Projects, activities, and investigations form an integral part of the learning process.

learning_outcomes.topic: Introduction to rational numbers, Representation of rational numbers on the number line, Density of rational numbers and its proof, Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers, Decimal representation of rational numbers
outcomes: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.

learning_outcomes.topic: Introduction to irrational numbers, Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3, The square root spiral
outcomes: Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Prove the irrationality of specific numbers (e.g., √2, √3).
Construct the square root spiral.
Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.

learning_outcomes.topic: Algebraic expressions, Definition of polynomial, Degree, Linear polynomials and applications
outcomes: Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.
Define a polynomial.
Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.
Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.

learning_outcomes.topic: Exploring linear patterns, Modelling linear growth and linear decay, Linear relationships, Visualising linear relationships, Slope and y-intercept of a line
outcomes: Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.
Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a linear equation in two variables.

learning_outcomes.topic: Introduction to sequences, Explicit or general rule of a sequence, Recursive rule of a sequence
outcomes: Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.
Identify the pattern in a sequence and predict the next few terms.
Determine the recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.

learning_outcomes.topic: Arithmetic Progressions (AP), Sum of the first n natural numbers, Geometric Progressions (GP)
outcomes: Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP) and determine the nth term.
Visualise an AP graphically.
Identify Geometric Progressions (GP) and determine the nth term.
Visualise a GP graphically.

learning_outcomes.topic: Applications of GP in fractals, Tower of Hanoi puzzle
outcomes: Analyse attributes of fractals using GP.
Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle.

learning_outcomes.topic: Revisiting algebraic identities, Visualising identities using geometrical models, Factorisation of algebraic expressions using identities
outcomes: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.
Determine the factors of algebraic expressions using identities.

learning_outcomes.topic: Finding new identities, Simplifying rational expressions
outcomes: Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.
Find simplified versions of rational expressions.

learning_outcomes.topic: Introduction to linear equations, Solution via graphical representation, Slope-intercept form
outcomes: Understand the concept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a pair of linear equations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Pair of linear equations, Graphical method for solving, Nature of solutions (consistency and inconsistency)
outcomes: Solve a pair of linear equations graphically.
Determine the nature of solutions of a pair of linear equations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Algebraic methods (substitution and elimination), Contextualised problems, Modelling daily-life phenomena
outcomes: Solve a pair of linear equations through the methods of substitution and elimination.
Model and solve contextualised problems using a pair of linear equations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Brief history, The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system, Distance between two points in the 2-D plane, Midpoint of the line-segment
outcomes: Specify locations and position of one point relative to another using coordinates.
Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.

learning_outcomes.topic: Distance, Midpoint, Collinearity check, Right-angled triangle verification
outcomes: Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.
Determine whether three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.
Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment using coordinates.

learning_outcomes.topic: Application and verification via computational thinking
outcomes: Check whether a triangle is right-angled using coordinates.
Apply computational thinking to model situations on the coordinate plane.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: History of geometry, Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Discovering Euclid's definitions, Axioms
outcomes: Describe how geometry grew from practical needs.
Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Euclidean geometry postulates, Parallelism, Construction of a square (Sulbasutras)
outcomes: State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.
Define parallelism of straight lines.

learning_outcomes.topic: Rays, angles, Intersecting lines, Pairs of angles
outcomes: Explain the notion of an angle and a ray.
Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems on intersecting lines (linear pair, vertically opposite), Parallelism theorems
outcomes: State and apply the linear pair theorem.
Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.

learning_outcomes.topic: Parallel lines, Transitivity of parallelism, Triangle angle properties
outcomes: Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.
Explain transitivity of parallelism.

learning_outcomes.topic: Practical applications, Proofs of conditions of congruence (SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS)
outcomes: Explain that a triangle is rigid.
Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.

learning_outcomes.topic: Isosceles triangles, Propositions and their converse
outcomes: Prove the basic properties of isosceles triangles.
Explain the notion of a proposition and its converse.

learning_outcomes.topic: SSA exceptions, Counter examples
outcomes: Explain why SSA is not generally a valid congruence condition.
Identify situations where SSA is valid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Properties of parallelograms, Midpoint theorem, Central symmetry
outcomes: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoint theorem applications, Medians concurrency, Tiling plane
outcomes: Prove the midpoint theorem.
Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons.

learning_outcomes.topic: Definitions (centre, diameter, radius), Chords, Angles subtended by arcs
outcomes: State the definition of a circle.
Explain meanings of terms like chord, arc, segment.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors of chords, Distance of chords from centre
outcomes: Explain why a unique circle exists through three non-collinear points.
Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.

learning_outcomes.topic: Angles subtended by arcs, Cyclic quadrilaterals
outcomes: Explain relationships between chords and angles at the centre.
Determine when four given points are concyclic.

learning_outcomes.topic: Perimeter of shapes, Introduction to Pi (irrationality), Length of an arc
outcomes: Define perimeter as the length around the boundary.
Explain that circumference-to-diameter ratio is constant.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heron's formula, Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals
outcomes: Use Heron's formula to compute the area of a triangle.
State Brahmagupta’s formula for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral.

learning_outcomes.topic: Squaring a rectangle, Area derivation (circle), Sector areas
outcomes: Explain the classical problem of 'squaring' a given shape.
Compute the area of a circle using the formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Cuboids, Cubes, Cylinders, Cones, Pyramids, Spheres (Hemispheres)
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of cuboid, cube, cylinder, cone, pyramid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Spheres (including hemispheres), Computational thinking in calculation strategies
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a sphere.
Use computational thinking to systematically calculate, and compare surface areas and volumes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Graphical representation (stacked bar graphs), Measures of central tendency (Mean, Median, Mode)
outcomes: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question.

learning_outcomes.topic: Weighted average, Interpretation of graphs
outcomes: Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.
Read and interpret stacked bar graphs.

learning_outcomes.topic: Concept of probability, Randomness, The probability scale
outcomes: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.

learning_outcomes.topic: Empirical vs Theoretical probability, Sample space and events
outcomes: Estimate the empirical probability of an event.
Define theoretical probability of an event.

learning_outcomes.topic: Tree diagrams, Tables for events
outcomes: Compute probability of events with the help of tree diagrams and tables.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions and Manipulation Activities
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras; Construct different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons. Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.

activities.title: Fractal Analysis using GP
activities.details: Analyse attributes of fractals using Geometric Progressions. Visualise a GP graphically.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.

projects.title: Continuous and Holistic Assessment Projects
projects.details: Projects, activities, and investigations form an integral part of the learning process. Portfolio (05 Marks) is a component of Internal Assessment.

practicals.title: Lab Practical (Mathematics Laboratory)
practicals.details: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books. Prescribed Books: Mathematics - Textbook for class IX, Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX, Laboratory Manual - Mathematics

Area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres

Summative assessment on polynomial operations and identities.

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Tower of Hanoi puzzle and Fractal analysis activities.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Summative assessment on sequence rules and applications.
Summative assessment on factorisation using identities.

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Graph plotting and real-life modelling scenarios.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Summative assessment on solving pairs of equations.

lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Summative assessment on postulates and basic proofs.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very High (Congruence conditions, Isosceles properties), Moderate (Propositions/Converses)
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Construction of triangles and verification of congruence.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Summative assessment on proving triangle congruence.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very High (Parallelogram characterisations, Midpoint theorem), Moderate (Symmetry)
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Paper manipulation for symmetry and tiling.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very High (Chord-Angle relationships, Cyclic points), Moderate (Definitions)
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Construction of circumcircles and chord properties.
Derivation of circle area and squaring shapes.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High (Central tendency, Weighted average), Moderate (Graphical representation)
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Stacked bar graph creation and data analysis.
